'''Paul Cook''' (born 23 July 1976) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer and [[Coach (sport)|coach]]. He represented [[England national rugby league team|England]] at the [[1995 Rugby League World Cup]], and played at club level for [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]], [[Bradford Bulls]] and [[Huddersfield Giants]], as a {{rlp|fb|wg|ce}} or {{rlp|so}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> and has coached at amateur level at Milford Marlins and [[Bramley Buffaloes]].

==Playing career==
Cook started his professional career at [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] before being transferred to [[Bradford Bulls]] in December 1995 in exchange for [[Carl Hall (rugby)|Carl Hall]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Bradford are ebullient as Cook and Hall trade places |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/bradford-are-ebullient-as-cook-and-hall-trade-places-1525722.html |website=The Independent |access-date=22 March 2025 |date=14 December 1995}}</ref> He played for Bradford Bulls on the wing in the [[1996 Challenge Cup]] Final, kicking six goals in their defeat by [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]].<ref>{{cite web|title=steveprescottfoundation.co.uk|url=http://www.steveprescottfoundation.co.uk/About/StevePrescottStats.aspx|work=Steve Prescott Stats|publisher=Steve Prescott Foundation|accessdate=3 June 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120808052156/http://www.steveprescottfoundation.co.uk/About/StevePrescottStats.aspx|archive-date=8 August 2012|url-status=dead}}</ref> 

In May 1997, he was signed by [[Huddersfield Giants]] for a fee of £70,000.<ref>{{cite web |title=Rugby League: Cook the latest to join Giants |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-cook-the-latest-to-join-giants-1262884.html |website=The Independent |access-date=22 March 2025 |date=21 May 1997}}</ref> He helped the club win promotion to the [[Super League]], and played in the club's 18–0 win over [[Hull F.C.|Hull]] in the [[1997 Rugby League Divisional Premiership|1997 Divisional Premiership]].

He went on to play for [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet Hawks]], scoring a try in their 12–11 win over [[Dewsbury Rams]] in the [[1999 Northern Ford Premiership]] Grand Final.

Cook won [[Cap (sport)|cap]]s for [[England national rugby league team|England]] while at Leeds in the [[1995 Rugby League World Cup]] against Fiji (sub), and South Africa.

==Coaching career==
Cook began coaching in 2001 at [[National Conference League]] side Milford Marlins,<ref>{{cite web |title=From tiny Acorns... |url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/news/7935354.from-tiny-acorns/ |website=York Press |access-date=22 March 2025 |date=1 November 2001}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Cook has the right recipe for success |url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/news/7929051.cook-has-the-right-recipe-for-success/ |website=York Press |access-date=22 March 2025 |date=26 March 2002}}</ref> and later became head coach at [[Bramley Buffaloes]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Cook guides Buffaloes to final |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/leeds/content/articles/2006/09/27/sport_buffaloes_grand_final_2006_feature.shtml |website=BBC |access-date=22 March 2025}}</ref> In 2008, he joined the coaching staff at [[Huddersfield Giants]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Cook becomes a Giant |url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/3832652.amp/ |website=Bradford Telegraph and Argus |access-date=22 March 2025 |date=9 November 2008}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Cook extends contract with Giants |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/huddersfield/8298808.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=22 March 2025 |date=9 October 2009}}</ref> He returned to the amateur game in 2012, re-joining Milford Marlins as coach.<ref>{{cite web |title=Cook returns to Marlins |url=https://www.pitchero.com/clubs/milfordmarlins/news/cook-returns-to-marlins-795924.html |website=Milford Marlins ARLFC |access-date=22 March 2025 |date=10 December 2012}}</ref>
